prediction.attackType: T1110.004 Credential Stuffing leading to T1078 Valid Accounts
prediction.summary: A credential stuffing attack targeted the 'user@corp' account, attempting to log in via both web (nginx) and SSH services from multiple distinct IP addresses. After a series of failed attempts, an attacker successfully authenticated to both the web application and SSH service from IP 198.51.100.123. An internal audit system confirmed distributed credential reuse, indicating a successful account compromise.

timeline.event: Failed SSH password attempt for user@corp from 198.51.100.12
Failed SSH password attempt for user@corp from 198.51.100.33
Failed SSH password attempt for user@corp from 198.51.100.44
Failed SSH password attempt for user@corp from 198.51.100.88
Failed SSH password attempt for user@corp from 198.51.100.101
Successful SSH password authentication for user@corp from 198.51.100.123
Audit system detected distributed credential reuse across 8 source IPs for account user@corp

recommendations: Immediately disable the 'user@corp' account and force a password reset. Revoke all active sessions for this account on both web and SSH services.
Block the attacker's source IP address 198.51.100.123 at the network perimeter (firewall/security group).
Investigate post-login activity for 'user@corp' on the 'web' host to identify any commands executed, files accessed, or further lateral movement by the attacker.

Review Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) logs for 'user@corp' to determine if MFA was enabled and bypassed, or if it was not in use. Implement or strengthen MFA for all accounts, especially for SSH and web access.
Analyze logs for other accounts that may have been targeted by the same distributed attack pattern and implement rate-limiting for login attempts on both SSH and web services.
